title: Create Event Ledger foundation
description: Introduce unified Event Ledger types/schemas and server storage at .fabric/events.jsonl. Add constants, append/read helpers, stable event envelope, typed event discriminators for existing ledger/audit concepts, and correlation/session id fields where appropriate. Do not migrate callers yet except tests for the new service.
test: Unit tests for event schema parse, append/read JSONL, malformed-line tolerance, and .fabric/events.jsonl path constant.
acceptance_criteria: New Event Ledger service writes and reads .fabric/events.jsonl; existing legacy files are untouched; typed events cover rule_context_planned, rule_selection, rule_sections_fetched, edit_intent_checked, rule_drift_detected, rule_baseline_accepted, mcp_event; tests pass.
hints: Use existing ledger-entry/audit-log schema style and read-ledger JSONL tolerance patterns || packages/shared/src/schemas/ledger-entry.ts;packages/server/src/services/read-ledger.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.ts;packages/server/src/services/_shared.ts
execution_directives: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test -- --runInBand || true; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- event-ledger --runInBand
prev_context:
[Explore event-ledger-storage] Current storage is dual JSONL: .fabric/.intent-ledger.jsonl plus legacy fallback for LedgerEntry, and .fabric/audit.jsonl for get_rules/edit_intent/rule_selection. HTTP MCP EventStore also writes kind=mcp-event lines into the ledger file. /api/ledger and history replay consume readLedger; /events watches ledger/meta/lock/forensic, not audit. Migration needs .fabric/events.jsonl plus compatibility projections for ledger views, audit compliance windows, MCP replay, and SSE semantics.
  Key files: packages/server/src/services/_shared.ts;packages/server/src/services/read-ledger.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.ts;packages/server/src/http.ts;packages/server/src/api/ledger.ts;packages/server/src/api/events.ts;packages/server/src/services/rehydrate-state.ts;packages/shared/src/schemas/ledger-entry.ts;packages/shared/src/schemas/events.ts;packages/server/src/services/read-ledger.test.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.test.ts;packages/cli/__tests__/ledger-append.test.ts;packages/dashboard/src/hooks/use-events.ts;packages/dashboard/src/views/intent-timeline.tsx;packages/dashboard/src/views/history-replay.tsx
[Explore mcp-instrumentation] MCP registration is split across packages/server/src/tools/* and calls services directly. Existing writes are service-local: getRules appends get_rules audit, getRuleSections appends rule_selection, appendIntent writes ledger then edit_intent audit; planContext is readOnlyHint true but has no event write, updateRegistry has none. Implement shared recordEvent for .fabric/events.jsonl and instrument plan_context_requested, rule_sections_selected, intent_recorded/edit_intent_checked, deprecated tool usage with correlation/session ids.
  Key files: packages/server/src/index.ts;packages/server/src/tools/plan-context.ts;packages/server/src/tools/rule-sections.ts;packages/server/src/tools/append-intent.ts;packages/server/src/tools/update-registry.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.ts;packages/server/src/services/get-rules.ts;packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts;packages/server/src/services/rule-sections.ts;packages/server/src/services/append-intent.ts;packages/server/src/services/update-registry.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.test.ts;packages/server/src/services/rule-sections.test.ts;packages/server/src/services/update-registry.test.ts
